name: LabVIEW
description: A graphical programming environment for instrument control, data acquisition, and laboratory automation. The standard for controlling physics experiments.
whyUseIt: LabVIEW uses a visual programming language (block diagrams instead of text code) to interface with lab instruments — oscilloscopes, function generators, multimeters, and custom sensors. It is the de facto standard for experimental physics automation.
fieldPosition: Experimental physics — laboratory instrument control and data acquisition
physicistWorkflow: Wire up a block diagram to read sensor data, process it in real time, display results, and log to file.
supervisorExpects: Ability to create LabVIEW programs that automate data collection and provide real-time feedback.
computationalImpact: Automates tedious manual measurements, enabling overnight data collection and precise timing control.

vocabulary.definition: A LabVIEW program — consisting of a front panel (UI) and block diagram (logic)
A standard interface bus for connecting lab instruments to a computer

beginnerMistakes: Creating spaghetti wiring instead of using subVIs for modularity
Not implementing proper error handling in instrument communication
